last_result.result: EasyCrypt rejected the committed tactic. Use the error summary and current goal to revise the proof step.
last_result.effect: The manager attempted to commit a tactic; use the result and latest goal to decide what changed.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

moves.title: Named-call route
moves.tactic_shape: exlim n{2}, ROin.m{1}, ROout.m{1} => n0 mr0 ms0; call (equ_cc n0 mr0 ms0).
moves.applicability: This lemma-call shape is not a complete tactic yet; determine the missing module, procedure, or term arguments from the current goal before probing it.
moves.runnable_status: Not yet; fill the missing EasyCrypt lemma, module, procedure, or term arguments before probing or committing this tactic.
missing_input: Concrete arguments required by the lemma or call form.
how_to_complete: Match the lemma or call statement against the visible call sites, module parameters, globals, and hypotheses in the current goal.
If the arity or argument order is unclear, inspect the tactic form or lookup the symbol before guessing.

limitations: The call still needs concrete EasyCrypt arguments before it is a tactic to probe or commit.

moves.tactic_shape: call (_: ={n} /\ c2{1} = c1{2} /\ n{1} = n{2} /\ a{1} = a{2} /\ p0{1} = p0{2} /\ n{1} = p{1}.`1 /\ ! (p{1}.`1 \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem. lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\ forall (n0 : nonce) (c3 : C.counter), (n0, c3) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n0 \in p{1}.`1 :: BNR.lenc{1} /\ (mr0 = SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} /\ ms0 = SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1}) /\ nap{2} = p0{2} /\ (n{2}, a{2}, p1{2}) = nap{2} /\ p1{1} = p0{1} /\ k{1} = Mem.k{1} /\ nap{1} = p1{1} /\ (n{1}, a{1}, p2{1}) = nap{1} /\ p0{2} = p{2} /\ p0{1} = p{1} /\ p{1} = p{2} /\ forall (n0 : nonce) (c3 : C.counter), (n0, c3) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n0 \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ check_plaintext BNR.lenc{1} p{1}).

application_context.how_to_use: Use this section when constructing the concrete invariant inside `call (_: ...)`. This section is not a tactic. It is a checklist for constructing the concrete invariant before using `probe_tactic` or `commit_tactic`.

inspect_if_unsure.why: After you draft a concrete invariant, this preview shows the obligations it creates and can reveal missing facts or extra conjuncts that make the proof harder.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: TACTIC REJECTED: `call (equ_cc _ mr0 ms0).`. Reason: proof-state analysis knows the lemma signature has explicit module, type, value, or memory slots.

proof-state analysis layer is call_site: 2 call site(s) and 3 callable lemma handle(s) are live (chacha_enc1, chacha_enc2, equ_cc). `inline *` is a broad global transformation here and can hide those handles; named calls and targeted inline are route context for compari...
Some imported or cross-scope handles may need lookup if that route is being tested; use lookup_candidates for the concrete symbol instead of treating the whole list as a to-do queue.
